sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Asian markets display mixed sentiment as geopolitical tensions ease while inflation concerns persist. The dollar maintains strength against major currencies, with USD/JPY approaching critical intervention levels near 158. European equities closed sharply lower on energy sector weakness, while Asian indices show resilience led by Japan's Nikkei gaining 0.60%. Gold continues its rally above $4700 as safe-haven demand persists amid central bank uncertainty.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
1. USD (+0.08% vs CHF, +0.05% vs JPY) - Dollar strength on yield differentials
2. CHF - Safe haven flows supporting franc stability
3. GBP (-0.01% vs USD) - Neutral positioning ahead of BoE guidance
4. EUR (-0.05% vs USD) - ECB dovish tilt weighing on single currency
5. CAD (-0.04% vs USD) - Oil weakness pressuring loonie
6. AUD (-0.04% vs USD) - RBA pause expectations limiting upside
7. NZD (-0.07% vs USD) - Weakest major on dovish RBNZ outlook

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1738: Bearish bias below 1.1750 resistance. ECB policy divergence with Fed supporting dollar strength. Next support at 1.1700 psychological level.
USD/JPY 157.66: Critical zone approaching 158 intervention threshold. BoJ verbal warnings intensifying. Momentum favors further upside but intervention risk caps gains.
GBP/USD 1.3538: Range-bound between 1.3500-1.3600. UK inflation data this week key catalyst. Cable consolidating ahead of BoE policy clarity.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Dollar demand from real money accounts on yield pickup
• JPY intervention speculation limiting speculative positioning above 157.50
• EUR shorts building on ECB dovish pivot expectations
• Commodity currency weakness on growth concerns weighing CAD/AUD

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: Asian outperformance: Nikkei 225 leads at 63119 (+0.60%) on weaker yen benefits. US futures mixed with Nasdaq underperforming at 26088 (-0.71%) on tech rotation concerns. European weakness evident: DAX declined 1.62% to 23955, CAC 40 down 0.95% to 7980. Defensive rotation favoring Dow Jones (+0.11% to 49761). Energy sector drag weighing on European indices while Japanese exporters benefit from currency tailwinds.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold $4713.20 (+0.57%): Bullish momentum above $4700 resistance. Central bank uncertainty and geopolitical premiums supporting precious metals complex.
WTI Crude $101.00 (-1.15%): Bearish reversal from $102 resistance. Demand concerns outweighing supply constraints. Key support at $100 psychological level.
Silver $87.66 (+2.42%): Outperforming gold on industrial demand recovery. Technical breakout above $85 targeting $90.
Natural Gas $2.819 (-0.84%): Seasonal weakness persisting. Storage build weighing on prompt contracts.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC $81208 (+0.91%): Bitcoin reclaiming $81000 after recent consolidation. Resistance at $82500, support holding at $79000. Institutional flows remaining constructive.
ETH $2300.81 (+1.16%): Ethereum showing relative strength. ETH/BTC ratio improving at 0.0283. Staking yields supporting demand above $2250.
Top Movers: Zcash leads at $585.40 (+2.61%), Dogecoin $0.1117 (+1.49%), Solana $95.51 (+1.31%) on altcoin rotation.

sections.heading: 📈 LONG
sections.content:
• Gold above $4710 targeting $4750
• Nikkei 225 momentum play above 63000
• Bitcoin breakout above $81500
• Silver continuation above $87
📉 SHORT
• WTI Crude below $101 targeting $99
• EUR/USD below 1.1730 targeting 1.1700
• European indices on energy weakness
• Natural Gas below $2.85

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: UK inflation data and wage growth metrics. US retail sales and industrial production. ECB officials speaking on policy outlook. 10-year Treasury yields at 4.35% key technical level to monitor.
